task.title: Summarizing and aggregating TCP traffic sent to specific subnets

context.p: The tcpsubnet utility summarizes and aggregates IPv4 TCP traffic that the local host sends to subnets and displays the output on a fixed interval. The utility uses eBPF features to collect and summarize the data to reduce the overhead.
By default, tcpsubnet summarizes traffic for the following subnets:
Note that the last subnet (0.0.0.0/0) is a catch-all option. The tcpsubnet utility counts all traffic for subnets different from the first four in this catch-all entry.
Follow the procedure to count the traffic for the 192.0.2.0/24 and 198.51.100.0/24 subnets. Traffic to other subnets will be tracked in the 0.0.0.0/0 catch-all subnet entry.

step.cmd: Start monitoring the amount of traffic sent to the 192.0.2.0/24, 198.51.100.0/24, and other subnets:

info.p: This command displays the traffic in bytes for the specified subnets once per second.

step.cmd: Press Ctrl+C to stop the tracing process.
